overall_assessment.fls_score_context: Your 417 FLS score represents solid intermediate performance with excellent knot technique but room for efficiency gains.
overall_assessment.session_headline: Strong surgeon's knot execution with proper hand switching, focus next on speed optimization and mark precision.

rubric_insights.observation: 180-second completion time is significantly slower than your recent 42-88 second range, indicating possible hesitation or overthinking
rubric_insights.impact_on_score: Approximately 180+ points lost due to extended time penalty

rubric_insights.observation: 1.5mm deviation from each mark (3mm total penalty) shows good but not excellent targeting accuracy
Excellent technique with proper surgeon's knot followed by alternating single throws with hand switching
rubric_insights.impact_on_score: No penalties - this was executed at proficiency level

technique_coaching.observation: In frame 2, needle loading appears methodical but slow - taking 20 seconds for initial setup suggests overthinking the grasp point
technique_coaching.why_it_matters: Efficient needle loading sets the rhythm for the entire task and builds confidence for subsequent phases
technique_coaching.correction: Load needle at 2/3 point from tip in one smooth motion - avoid repositioning once grasped
technique_coaching.drill: Practice 20 rapid needle loads in 2 minutes, focusing on single-motion pickup at correct grasp point

technique_coaching.observation: 50-second suture placement phase (frames 3-5) indicates excessive repositioning or hesitation during tissue passage
technique_coaching.why_it_matters: Smooth, confident tissue passage prevents trauma and maintains surgical flow
technique_coaching.correction: Plan your needle path before entry - commit to the trajectory and drive through in one smooth arc
technique_coaching.drill: Practice 15 rapid suture placements through foam targets, aiming for sub-15 seconds per placement

technique_coaching.observation: Excellent surgeon's knot technique in frames 6-7, but 30-second duration suggests room for speed optimization without sacrificing quality
technique_coaching.why_it_matters: Your knot technique is already proficient - now focus on maintaining quality while increasing speed
technique_coaching.correction: Maintain your current hand switching pattern but reduce hesitation between loop formations
technique_coaching.drill: Practice 10 surgeon's knots with alternating throws, target 15 seconds total knot time

strengths_to_reinforce.observation: Perfect surgeon's knot execution in frames 6-7 with proper double throw technique and excellent tension control
strengths_to_reinforce.why_it_matters: This is advanced technique that many trainees struggle with - you've mastered the fundamentals
strengths_to_reinforce.encouragement: Your knot technique is at proficiency level - this foundation will serve you well as you focus on speed

strengths_to_reinforce.observation: Consistent hand switching pattern through frames 7-9 shows excellent understanding of proper knot security principles
strengths_to_reinforce.why_it_matters: Proper hand switching prevents knot slippage and demonstrates surgical maturity
strengths_to_reinforce.encouragement: Your alternating throw technique is textbook perfect - maintain this pattern as you work on efficiency

practice_plan.session_focus: Speed optimization while maintaining your excellent knot technique
practice_plan.warmup_drill: 5 minutes of rapid needle loading practice - 20 loads in 2 minutes

primary_drill.name: Timed Suture Placement Sprint
primary_drill.description: Set up 5 practice targets. Load needle, place suture, tie surgeon's knot + 2 alternating throws, cut. Focus on smooth transitions between phases without rushing individual techniques.
primary_drill.success_criteria: Complete each rep in under 90 seconds while maintaining your current knot quality

practice_plan.cooldown: Review which phase felt most rushed - that's your next focus area
practice_plan.estimated_sessions_to_improvement: 3-4 focused sessions to reach sub-120 second consistency

progress_context.vs_baseline: Significant improvement from early 28-point attempt, knot technique has advanced dramatically
progress_context.vs_peak: This 180s time is much slower than your recent 42-88s range - suggests possible fatigue or equipment issues
progress_context.milestone_note: Target sub-120 seconds with current knot quality to break into advanced scoring range

coaching_limitations: Camera angle limits assessment of needle entry angles and exact tissue trauma. Suture tension during knot tying not fully visible from this perspective.
